text.title: tool-use-function-calling Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for tool-use-function-calling as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Passing all 50+ available tools to every LLM call.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Token cost grows linearly with tool count. At 50 tools, selection accuracy drops significantly. Use RAG-over-tools: embed all tool descriptions, retrieve the top 5–10 by cosine similarity to the user query.

failure-mode.description: Accepting free-text from LLM for parameters that should be typed (e.g., user_id as unvalidated string).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs hallucinate IDs and enum values. Always validate against a known set or fetch from a prior tool call result; never trust a naked identifier generated from thin air.

failure-mode.description: Silent success on tool failure — returning {} or None instead of an error object.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The LLM interprets an empty result as success and continues reasoning on missing data, producing confident but wrong answers. Explicit error codes allow the model to stop and report the problem.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A tool returning unexpected output or an LLM stuck in a reasoning loop causes unbounded API calls and cost. Cap at 10–20 iterations and surface a max-iterations error.
